other_observers: none; I am the original finder. Several other birders saw my post on co-birds and were able to observe the bird. I do not rember all the names, but they posted in the May 14-17 time frame.
specific_location: SW side of Marston Reservoir adjacent to Twin Shores Subdivision and small Open Space area.
description: Light gray bird with almost white head and very long black and white tail. When perched, faint salmon color on the belly. When the bird sallied out to flycatch, the bright pink/orange underwing coverts were very conspicious as well as a black and white pattern on the tail. Overall size and shape (except for the long tail) were similar to Western kingbird when perched on fence wire.Behaviors: The bird was perched on a wire fence separating Marston Reservoir from the houses in Twin Shore Subdivision. He flew out to fly catch, then returned to the same perch or close by. I observed the bird sally out and return to the fence. I also observed the bird use a small bush (4 -5 feet tall) as a perch to sally forth.  The area was somewhat open with a road adjacent to to Marston Lake, fence line, open space area with small trees/shrubs, and a cattail marsh. Small grove of trees about 100 yards distant.Call: did not callPlumage: adult plumage
